core_mechanism: deriving a system's governing field equations from a variational (stationary-action) principle in which the Lagrangian functional is generalized from a linear term to an arbitrary nonlinear function of a single fundamental scalar invariant
brief_reason: The abstract centers on 'actions, field equations' for the class of f(R) theories, i.e. replacing the linear curvature scalar in the action by an arbitrary function f(R) and extremizing that action to obtain the equations of motion, with 'equivalence with other theories' arising from field-redefinition of the same functional.
